current.name: Dr. Kieran Samuk
current.title: Principal Investigator
current.bio_html: Kieran is an Assistant Professor in the EEOB Department at UC Riverside. He is primarily interested in evolutionary genetics, particularly the interaction between recombination and the forces of evolution.

current.name: Dr. Maria Akopyan
current.title: Postdoc, 2025 – Present
current.bio_html: Maria is an NSF PRFB postdoc co-advised by Dr. Ellie Armstrong. She applies genomic resources to reconstruct demographic histories, characterize recombination landscapes and structural variation, and investigate the genomic architecture of adaptation.

current.name: Dr. Natalia Rivera-Rincon
current.title: Postdoc, 2025 – Present
current.bio_html: Natalia is interested in how recombination and chromosomal inversions shape genome architecture and facilitate adaptation. She is particularly fascinated by the role of structural variation in mediating responses to environmental change and climatic gradients.

current.name: Gina Lucas
current.title: PhD Student, 2022 – Present
current.bio_html: Gina is interested in evolutionary genetics and genomics. Her current project is focused on understanding the role of gene flow in shaping adaptation and diversity in natural populations.

current.name: Alexandra Sumarli
current.title: PhD Student, 2022 – Present
current.bio_html: Prior to joining the lab, Alex worked at the USGS and completed an MSc at SDSU working on the phylogenomics of chuckwallas.

current.name: Mikhail Plaza
current.title: PhD Student, 2023 – Present
current.bio_html: Mikhail is interested in genome evolution and its applications in plant breeding and adaptation. Before joining the lab, he earned an MSc from CSUN.

current.name: Rachel Ford-Espinoza
current.title: Undergraduate, 2024 – Present
current.bio_html: Rachel is a fourth-year undergraduate pursuing a degree in biology. She is assisting in the stickleback and fruit fly research in the lab.

current.name: Ly Tran
current.title: Undergraduate, 2024 – Present
current.bio_html: Ly (they/any) is an undergraduate in the Neuroscience program at UCR, and is broadly interested in evolutionary biology. They are a NIH MARC-U Scholar. Their project is focused on understanding the genetic basis of albinism in threespine sticklebacks.

current.name: Adithi Sesani
current.title: Undergraduate, 2024 – Present
current.bio_html: Adithi is an undergraduate in the Neuroscience program at UCR. She is currently working on recombination rate variation in natural populations of Arabidopsis.

current.name: Emme Tran
current.title: Undergraduate, 2025 – Present
current.bio_html: Emme is an undergraduate in the Neuroscience program at UCR. She is assisting in projects related to the threespine stickleback.

current.name: Kimberly Mondragon-Arroyo
current.title: Undergraduate, 2025 – Present
current.bio_html: Kimberly is an undergraduate in the Biology program at UCR. She is assisting with research focused on the three-spine stickleback.

current.name: James Daluraya
current.title: Undergraduate, 2025 – Present
current.bio_html: James is an undergraduate in the Biology program at UCR. He is working on recombination rate variation in natural populations of Arabidopsis.
